data.description:

data.title: Shareholders who lost money on Civitas Resources, Inc. (CIVI) Should Contact Wolf Haldenstein
data.description:
NEW YORK, May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP, a distinguished law firm with over 125 years of history, informs investors that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Civitas Resources, Inc. ("Civitas " or the "Company") on behalf of a class consisting of all investors that purchased or otherwise acquired Civitas securities between February 27, 2024 and February 24, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period").

Deadline to sign up:

If you are an investor who bought or otherwise acquired Civitas securities during the Class Period, you have until July 1, 2025, to ask the Court to appoint you as Lead Plaintiff for the class.

PLEASE CLICK HERE TO JOIN THE CASE AND SUBMIT CONTACT INFORMATION

What happened:

The filed complaint alleges that throughout the Class Period, the defendants made materially false, misleading and potentially damaging statements regarding the Business Operations of Civitas. These include unvalidated claims about the Company's status, capabilities, and prospects, which have had considerable adverse effects on investors who relied on the information shared.

On February 24, 2025, Civitas announced its Q4 and full-year 2024 financial results, which included revenue of $1.29 billion, missing consensus estimates by $3.44 million, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.78 for the quarter, missing consensus estimates by $0.21 per share. In addition, Civitas reported net income of $151.1 million, or $1.57 per share, compared with $302.9 million, or $3.23 per share, in the year-ago quarter.

Additionally, on February 24, Civitas outlined the Company's 2025 outlook, which noted that, compared to Q4 2024, "lower volumes are primarily driven by the DJ Basin, due to natural declines following peak production in the fourth quarter." In addition, Civitas announced a 10% reduction in its workforce across all levels.

Finally, the Company announced the termination of its Chief Operating Officer Hodge Walker and Chief Transformation Officer Jerome Kelly.

On this news, Civitas's stock price fell $8.95 per share, or 18%, to close at $40.35 per share on February 25, 2025

Why Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP?:

This illustrious firm, founded in 1888, is steadfast in their pursuit of justice for investors who have suffered financial harm due to these misrepresented statements. The law firm brings to the fore over 125 years of legal expertise in securities litigation and has a proven track record of protecting the rights of investors.

We encourage all investors who have been affected or have information that will assist in our investigation, to contact Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP.

Contact:

  • Phone: (800) 575-0735 or (212) 545-4774
  • Email: classmember@whafh.com
  • Contact Person: Gregory Stone, Director of Case and Financial Analysis

Firm Website: Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP

data.title: Princeton Bancorp, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Princeton Bancorp, Inc., a prominent financial institution, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust financial performance and strategic business operations, reflecting its resilience and growth in a dynamic economic environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $33.3 million, an increase of $5.2 million compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher loan balances and yields.
  • Net Interest Income: $18.8 million, up by $3.3 million from the first quarter of 2024, primarily due to higher interest income on loans and securities.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $268 thousand, reflecting an increase of $82 thousand compared to the first quarter of 2024.
  • Total Non-Interest Income: $2.2 million, an increase of $205 thousand or 10.3% from the prior year period.
  • Total Non-Interest Expense: $13.8 million, increased by $2.0 million or 16.5% due to higher salaries, data processing, and other expenses.
  • Net Income: $5.4 million, an increase from $4.3 million in the first quarter of 2024, primarily due to higher net interest income.
  • Earnings per Common Share - Diluted: $0.77, up from $0.68 in the first quarter of 2024, reflecting improved profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The commercial real estate portfolio is distributed across New York (45.3%), New Jersey (39.1%), and Pennsylvania (14.1%), with a small portion in other regions. The New York market remains the largest segment, although it saw a slight decrease in its share compared to the previous period.
  • Commercial Real Estate Portfolio: The CRE portfolio includes multi-family, owner-occupied, and non-owner-occupied properties. Multi-family properties constitute 37.4% of the portfolio, with a weighted average loan-to-value (LTV) of 53.0%. Owner-occupied properties make up 29.3% with a 36.2% LTV, while non-owner-occupied properties, including retail and office buildings, account for 31.3% of the portfolio.
  • Loan Portfolio Growth: The loan portfolio saw a net increase of $37.7 million, driven by a $29.2 million rise in residential mortgages and a $19.0 million increase in commercial real estate loans. This growth was partially offset by decreases in construction loans and commercial and industrial loans.
  • Non-Performing Assets: Non-performing assets decreased by $614 thousand, representing 1.43% of total loans, net of deferred fees and costs, as of March 31, 2025. This marks an improvement from the previous period.
  • Deposit Trends: Total deposits decreased by $22.0 million, with significant reductions in money market deposits and certificates of deposit. However, there was an increase in interest-bearing demand deposits and savings deposits.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued management of interest rate risk and credit risk, with a focus on maintaining a strong capital position. The economic environment remains uncertain, with potential impacts from geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures.

SEC Filing: Princeton Bancorp, Inc. [ BPRN ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nabors Industries Ltd, a leading provider of advanced drilling solutions for the oil and gas industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting the impact of recent acquisitions and market conditions on its business segments.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Revenues: $736.2 million, representing a decrease of $2.5 million compared to the three months ended March 31, 2024.
  • Net Income Attributable to Nabors: $33.0 million, a $67.3 million increase from a net loss of $34.3 million in the prior year period, primarily due to a gain on bargain purchase related to the Parker acquisition.
  • Net Income Per Diluted Share: $2.18, compared to a net loss of $4.54 per diluted share for the three months ended March 31, 2024.
  • General and Administrative Expenses: $68.5 million, an increase of $6.8 million or 11%, reflecting increases in workforce costs and general operating costs due to the Parker acquisition and inflationary pressures.
  • Depreciation and Amortization Expense: $154.6 million, a decrease of $3.0 million due to older assets reaching the end of their useful lives.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The U.S. Drilling segment experienced a 15% decrease in operating revenues due to a 13% decrease in the average number of rigs working, while pricing remained stable.
    • The International Drilling segment saw a 9% increase in operating revenues, driven by a 5% increase in average rigs working and improved pricing.
    • The Drilling Solutions segment reported a 23% increase in operating revenues, largely due to the acquisition of Parker operations.
    • The Rig Technologies segment experienced a 12% decrease in operating revenues, attributed to a decline in U.S. activity.
  • Geographical Performance:
    • U.S. drilling activity decreased, reflecting a reduction in rig counts due to market volatility and reduced capital availability.
    • International markets showed an expansion in production capacity and increased drilling activity, particularly in unconventional resources.
  • Sales Units:
    • The average number of rigs working in the U.S. decreased by 10.5 rigs, or 13%, while internationally, the average increased by 4 rigs, or 5%, indicating a shift in operational focus.
  • Future Outlook:
    • The demand for services and products is closely tied to oil and gas prices, which are influenced by supply and demand cycles and geopolitical uncertainties.
    • The company anticipates that recent reductions in U.S. interest rates may positively impact future rig demand.
    • Internationally, an increase in oilfield activity is expected due to expanded production capacity and unconventional resource development.
  • Recent Developments:
    • The acquisition of Parker Drilling Company was completed on March 11, 2025, contributing to increased revenues in the Drilling Solutions segment and impacting general and administrative expenses due to integration costs.

SEC Filing: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD [ NBR ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: JUNIPER NETWORKS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Juniper Networks Inc., a leading provider of networking solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by increased sales across all customer solutions and verticals. The company has also made strides in operational efficiency and cost management, contributing to a return to profitability.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $1,280.2 million, an 11% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher sales volume across all customer solutions and verticals.

Gross Margin: $754.7 million, representing 59.0% of net revenues, slightly down from 59.3% in 2024 due to an unfavorable revenue mix, partially offset by higher service revenue and lower inventory-related expenses.

Operating Income: $89.4 million, a significant improvement from an operating loss of $14.2 million in 2024, primarily due to lower share-based compensation and merger-related charges.

Net Income: $64.1 million, compared to a net loss of $0.8 million in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency and cost management.

Net Income Per Share: Basic and Diluted EPS of $0.19, compared to $0.00 in 2024, indicating a return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported an increase in net revenues across all customer solutions, with Wide Area Networking showing a 16% increase, Campus and Branch up by 22%, and Data Center growing by 9%. Hardware Maintenance and Professional Services also saw a 2% increase.

Geographical Performance: The Americas region, particularly the United States, experienced a significant 24% increase in net revenues. However, the EMEA region saw a decline of 7%, while the APAC region experienced a modest 5% growth.

Customer Segments: Revenue from the Cloud vertical increased by 29%, while the Enterprise segment grew by 12%. The Service Provider segment remained relatively flat with a slight decrease.

Software and Security Products: Software and related services revenue increased by 13%, driven by strong sales of software support and maintenance and SaaS contracts. Total security offerings saw a marginal increase of 1%.

Operational Efficiency: The company undertook efforts to improve product gross margins through performance and quality improvements, supply chain optimization, pricing management, and increased software and solution sales.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates that global economic conditions and uncertainties regarding tariffs may impact overall performance starting in the second quarter of 2025. Management is actively monitoring these factors to mitigate potential impacts on operations.

SEC Filing: JUNIPER NETWORKS INC [ JNPR ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: GREENWAY TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Greenway Technologies Inc., a company specializing in the development of proprietary gas-to-liquids (GTL) synthesis gas conversion systems, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities, reflecting its ongoing efforts in research and development, as well as strategic market positioning.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0, The company reported no revenues for the quarters ended March 31, 2025, and 2024.
  • Net Loss: $683,641, The net loss increased by $326,570 compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to increases in research and development expenses, legal expenses, consulting fees, travel expenses, and commission expenses.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $(0.00), The loss per share remained unchanged from the previous year, despite the increase in net loss.
  • Operating Expenses: $532,142, Operating expenses increased significantly due to higher general and administrative expenses and research and development costs.
  • Interest Expense: $151,499, Interest expense decreased slightly due to an adjustment in interest payable on one note payable.

Business Highlights

  • GTL Technology Development: Greenway Technologies, Inc. is focused on the research and development of proprietary gas-to-liquids (GTL) synthesis gas conversion systems. The company has developed the G-Reformer, a key component in converting natural gas into syngas, which is then used to produce fuels and chemicals. The company aims to become a significant producer of renewable GTL synthesized diesel and jet fuels, with a focus on U.S. market opportunities.
  • Patent and Licensing Agreements: The company holds several patents related to its GTL technology, including U.S. Patent 10,633,594 B1 for syngas generation. Additionally, Greenway Technologies has an exclusive worldwide patent licensing agreement with the University of Texas at Arlington for its natural gas reforming technologies.
  • Research and Development Activities: During the first quarter of 2025, the company expanded its research and development efforts, including an enhanced relationship with the University of Texas at Arlington and the purchase of a G-Reformer unit for $50,000.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is focused on executing business operations more fully in 2025 and exploring strategic and partnership opportunities to enhance its market presence and operational capabilities.
  • Market Position and Competition: Greenway Technologies operates in the GTL industry, which converts natural gas into high-quality liquid products. The company differentiates itself with its scalable and transportable GTL plants, designed to meet specific gas field production requirements. Key competitors include major industry players like Shell and Sasol, but Greenway's technology is tailored for smaller, distributed applications.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to raise additional funds through public or private offerings to support ongoing operations and further implement the business plan. The company is also focused on generating revenues from its GTL technology and expanding its market presence.
  • Employee Structure: As of the filing date, the company has three employees, with two under employment agreements. The company considers its employee relations to be satisfactory.

SEC Filing: GREENWAY TECHNOLOGIES INC [ GWTI ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: CB Financial Services, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CB Financial Services, Inc., a regional banking institution, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter, providing a detailed overview of its financial and operational performance. The report highlights the company's stable interest income, despite market fluctuations, and outlines strategic shifts in its business operations aimed at enhancing future profitability.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $17.847 million, reflecting a slight decrease from the previous year, indicating stable interest income despite market fluctuations.
  • Net Interest and Dividend Income: $11.311 million, showing a decrease compared to the previous year, highlighting the impact of increased interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $1.909 million, a significant decrease from the previous year's $4.196 million, primarily due to increased noninterest expenses and lower noninterest income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share (EPS): $0.37, down from $0.82 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.35, compared to $0.82 in the previous year, indicating a reduction in earnings available to shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company derives its income primarily from banking and bank-related services, including interest income on commercial, commercial mortgage, residential real estate, and consumer loan financing, as well as interest and dividend income on securities, insurance commissions, and fees generated from deposit services.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates nine offices in Greene, Allegheny, Washington, Fayette, and Westmoreland Counties in southwestern Pennsylvania, and three offices in Marshall and Ohio Counties in West Virginia, indicating a strong regional presence in these areas.
  • Sales Units: Loan production totaled $28.6 million, with $15.6 million of loans paid off since December 31, 2024, reflecting active lending operations.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on more profitable commercial products, as indicated by the discontinuation of indirect automobile loan production. This strategic shift is expected to impact future loan portfolio composition and profitability.

SEC Filing: CB Financial Services, Inc. [ CBFV ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ferrovial SE is €45.60​, below​ its last closing price of $48.92. ​​​

data.title: NATO's Rutte wants 2032 deadline for new defence spending goals, Dutch PM says
data.description:
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte has proposed that alliance members meet higher targets for defence spending by 2032, Dutch Prime Minister Dick Schoof said on Friday.

Reuters reported last week that Rutte had proposed NATO members raise defence spending to 3.5% of their GDP, and a further 1.5% on broader security-related items to meet U.S. President Donald Trump's demand for a 5% target.

Speaking to reporters after a cabinet meeting, Schoof confirmed those figures, which would represent a major increase from the current NATO goal of spending 2% of GDP on defence.

NATO aims to agree the new targets at a summit of alliance leaders including Trump, in The Hague, on June 24-25.

"Rutte has sent a letter to all NATO members to say that he expects that the commitment at the NATO summit will be 3.5% on hard military spending, to be reached in 2032, and 1.5% on related spending such as on infrastructure, cyber security, and similar things also to be reached by 2032," Schoof said.

NATO did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Schoof's remarks.

He said the government of the Netherlands, where Rutte was a long-serving prime minister, would continue discussions to determine its position on the proposal.

Other NATO members were also considering Rutte's request and would have to discuss it with their parliaments, he added.

Twenty-two of NATO's 32 members meet the current 2% target. But leaders across NATO say that goal is no longer sufficient as they now regard Russia as a far greater threat in the wake of its 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

No NATO country meets Trump's 5% target. According to NATO, the United States spends about 3.2% of GDP on defence. Poland spends the biggest share of its GDP - at over 4%.

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care
data.description:
Health care stocks declined Friday afternoon, with the NYSE Health Care Index and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) each decreasing 0.4%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) retreated 1.2%.

In corporate news, Apellis Pharmaceuticals shares were falling 3%. The company has had a difficult Q1 as charitable foundation funding gaps and weaker-than-expected Syfovre sales weigh on the outlook, BofA Securities said in a Friday report. BofA downgraded the stock to neutral from buy and cut the price target to $23 from $41.

data.title: Sector Update: Tech
data.description:
Tech stocks were mixed Friday afternoon, with the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) decreasing 0.2% and the SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD) adding 1.1%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor index rose 0.7%.

In sector news, tech industry representatives are warning that new US investments pledged by major firms could be put at risk if Congress doesn't fully reinstate a tax deduction for research and development, Bloomberg reported, citing the Information Technology Industry Council.

In corporate news, Nvidia-backed CoreWeave is in talks to raise $1.5 billion in debt after its IPO raised less than what was targeted, Bloomberg reported. Separately, Nvidia plans to release a reduced-performance version of its H20 AI chip for China by July, following US export restrictions on the original model, Reuters reported. Nvidia shares were shedding 0.8% and CoreWeave fell past 7%.

data.title: GCM Grosvenor Is Maintained at Outperform by Oppenheimer
Sector Update: Consumer
data.description:
Consumer stocks were mixed Friday afternoon, with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) decreasing 0.5% and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) up 0.2%.

In corporate news, Lyft shares jumped 24%. The company unexpectedly swung to a Q1 profit amid double-digit gains in gross bookings, while it also forecast continued growth for the key metric in the ongoing three-month period.

data.title: ProvisionAi Webinar: Cost Savings and Driving Success – Insights from Scott DeGroot
data.description:
FRANKLIN, Tenn., May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — ProvisionAi recently hosted an exclusive webinar titled "Cost Savings and Driving Success," featuring Scott DeGroot, former VP of Global Planning at Kimberly-Clark and current Adjunct Lecturer and Researcher at the University of Tennessee’s Haslam College of Business Global Supply Chain Institute. Moderated by Robert Bowman, Editor-in-Chief of SupplyChainBrain, the webinar offered participants key strategies and real-world insights into achieving significant supply chain improvements.

In this engaging discussion, DeGroot shared actionable lessons from his successful experiences implementing innovative supply chain strategies that reduced costs, enhanced operational efficiency, and enabled strategic reinvestment in advanced technological systems.

Key topics covered during the webinar included:

  • Obtaining IT support for supply chain transformation
  • Rapid deployment and leveraging AI-driven supply network planning solutions
  • Using initial successes to identify and seize further business opportunities

Participants gained practical insights into:

  • Data-Driven Cost Reduction: Utilizing analytics for increased efficiency and waste reduction.
  • AI-Driven Performative Analytics: Enhancing operational efficiency through AI solutions.
  • Strategic Reinvestment: Turning cost savings into investment opportunities for future innovations.

The initiatives discussed demonstrated notable benefits, including reduced transportation costs, optimized carrier utilization, improved On-Time In-Full (OTIF) delivery rates, and funded further investments in supply chain technology.

The recorded webinar is now available for viewing at SupplyChainBrain.

For more information about ProvisionAi and future webinars, please visit www.provisionai.com.

About ProvisionAi ProvisionAi provides advanced, AI-driven logistics solutions that dramatically improve supply chain performance, reduce costs, and enhance sustainability. Its innovative platforms empower companies to optimize logistics processes and achieve long-term operational excellence.

Media Contact:

Tom Moore

tom.moore@provisionai.com

Cell: +1 615 417-9591

ProvisionAi.com

Platinum Analytics Cayman's Platinum Singapore Provides FX Trading Software
Air-Traffic Controllers for Newark Experienced Another Outage. United Stock Falls. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

The Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed a second outage in the Newark airspace in two weeks.

"In a post on X, the FAA wrote: "The outage occurred around 3:55 a.m. on Friday, May 9, and lasted approximately 90 seconds." It added that the information was preliminary and subject to change.

"There was a telecommunications outage that impacted communications and radar display at Philadelphia TRACON Area C, which guides aircraft in and out of Newark Liberty International Airport airspace," the FAA added.

Delays at the airport for some arriving flights are currently four hours due to weather, according to the FAA.

Shares of United Airlines, which has a hub at Newark airport, were down more than 3.1% after the news.

The first blackout occurred on April 28. The administration announced plans for a new air-traffic control system on Thursday.

This is a breaking story. Check back for more news and analysis.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Gunmen kill 30 travellers in Nigeria's unstable southeast, Amnesty says
data.description:
Gunmen shot dead at least 30 travellers in an attack in Nigeria's southeastern Imo state, Amnesty International said on Friday, raising fresh concerns about violence in a region rife with insecurity.

More than 20 vehicles and trucks were set ablaze by the attackers, who were suspected to be members of the banned separatist Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB), Amnesty said in a post on X.

Imo police spokesperson Henry Okoye confirmed the attack occurred in Thursday's early hours, but declined comment on the number of fatalities. One of the assailants was killed by the police, Okoye told Reuters on Friday.

A police statement said the gunmen, operating in three groups, barricaded the highway at about 0400 GMT and shot sporadically before setting vehicles ablaze.

"A full-scale search and cordon operation is currently underway, with security operatives combing nearby forests and surrounding areas where the suspects are believed to be hiding," the police said in the statement.

IPOB campaigns for the secession of southeastern Nigeria, where the majority belong to the Igbo ethnic group. Nigerian authorities have labelled IPOB a terrorist organisation.

Civil war engulfed the Biafra region in the late 1960s, killing more than 1 million people.

Thursday's attack coincided with a visit by President Bola Tinubu to the region, occurring in the same week that IPOB leader Nnamdi Kanu appeared in federal court where he is facing trial on terrorism charges.

Amnesty called on Nigerian authorities to investigate the attack and bring the perpetrators to justice.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial
data.description:
Financial stocks were mixed in Friday afternoon trading, with the NYSE Financial Index fractionally higher and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) slightly lower.

The Philadelphia Housing Index was down 0.5%, while the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) added 0.5%.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) was up 1.5% at $103,020, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries was slightly lower at 4.37%.

In economic news, US-China trade talks are expected to start this weekend in Switzerland. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet China's Vice Premier He Lifeng in Geneva. The US imposed 145% tariffs on Chinese goods, and China retaliated with 125% tariffs against the US. President Donald Trump said Friday in a social media post that China should open its market to US goods, while an "80% Tariff on China seems right! Up to Scott B".

In corporate news, Coinbase shares fell 2.3%. The company reported Q1 adjusted earnings late Thursday of $1.94 per diluted share, down from $2.53 a year earlier and below the FactSet consensus estimate of $2.30.
